commands.review_summary:
The PR threads an assistant-attachment-loaded callback from WebChat media rendering to the app scroll scheduler and invokes it when assistant audio/video metadata loads.

PR surface: Source +27. Total +27 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main renders assistant media controls after async availability without scheduling a follow-up chat scroll. I did not run a live Safari or Chrome reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(webchat): keep audio/video attachments pinned to newest message after render
target.body:
Closes #96593

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where WebChat scroll position jumps to the middle of the conversation after an audio (or video) attachment finishes loading, forcing users to manually scroll back to the latest message.

## Why This Change Was Made

The chat scroll scheduler only runs when message, stream, loading, or tab state changes. Assistant attachments resolve availability asynchronously, and the rendered `<audio>` / `<video>` controls then change layout after the scheduler has already run. The fix wires the media `loadedmetadata` event back to `scheduleChatScroll()` so the chat re-evaluates whether it should stay near the bottom once the player renders. Users who have intentionally scrolled up are still respected because `scheduleChatScroll()` checks `chatUserNearBottom` and the distance from the bottom.

## User Impact

Audio and video replies in WebChat now stay pinned to the newest message after the player renders, matching the behavior of plain text replies.

## Evidence

- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` passes.
- UI unit tests for the touched surface:
  - `src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` — 119 passed
  - `src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts` — 31 passed
  - `src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` — 58 passed (1 unrelated locale-specific AM/PM failure)
